title: Does OVOSEC implement a Zero-Logs Policy?
subtitle: Our strict privacy standard and local compliance.
content: Yes, absolutely. OVOSEC is incorporated as a legal entity in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, and strictly adheres to local PDPA guidelines alongside global zero-knowledge compliance frameworks. Our open-source backend core operates strictly in volatile RAM memory. We do not collect, monitor, track, or archive your network destinations, query history, payload contents, or real-world source IP allocation logs. Your digital presence is completely anonymous.

title: What level of encryption does the OVOSEC infrastructure enforce?
subtitle: Advanced cryptographic protocols and security standards.
content: Every packet transmitted via our secure relay network is encapsulated using industry-standard AES-256-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305 cryptographic primitives, layered with modern TLS handshakes. This cryptographic architecture guarantees absolute end-to-end secrecy, nullifying any potential middleman tampering or packet eavesdropping even when accessing corporate resources via unsecured public Wi-Fi access points.

title: What is OVOSEC zero-knowledge log policy?
subtitle: Understanding our commitment to security and zero user tracking.
content: We adhere to a strict Zero-Logs Policy. We do not monitor, capture, store, or sell any information regarding your browsing history, traffic destination, data payload contents, or DNS requests. For comprehensive legal terms, please read our full Privacy Policy.

title: How does OVOSEC secure public Wi-Fi transmissions?
subtitle: Military-grade encryption and protocol suites on public gateways.
content: All network traffic routed through OVOSEC secure nodes is encrypted via state-of-the-art AES-256-GCM and ChaCha20-Poly1305 primitives. This guarantees that your data packet streams remain protected from eavesdropping or Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks even on untrusted public Wi-Fi hotspots.

title: Does OVOSEC protect my credentials and personal data when connecting via unsecured public Wi-Fi?
subtitle: Preventing data theft and MitM attacks on public hot-spots.
content:
Yes, completely. When OVOSEC is active (especially with TUN Mode enabled), the underlying core architecture encapsulates all local outbound traffic into a strictly encrypted cryptographic tunnel (utilizing AEAD primitives).

Even if the public Wi-Fi hotspot is compromised or running a "Man-in-the-Middle" packet sniffer, attackers can only see scrambled, unbreakable metadata. Your plaintext passwords, financial transactions, and communication payloads remain completely bulletproof.

title: Why does my local antivirus or Windows Defender flag OVOSEC when enabling TUN Mode?
subtitle: Handling antivirus false positives for system network changes.
content:
This is a standard false positive. Because TUN Mode creates an advanced enterprise-grade virtual network adapter at the operating system level to encrypt all outbound background data, strict local security firewalls may flag this deep-network modification as suspicious.

OVOSEC is completely open-source, heavily audited, and malware-free. You can safely add the OVOSEC application directory to your antivirus Exclusion / Trust List to ensure uninterrupted network optimization.
